name: Cortagen
subtitle: AEDP | Brain Cortex Bioregulator Peptide

molecular.halfLife: Minutes (short peptide); effects persist via epigenetic changes

overview: Cortagen is a Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (AEDP) with primary effects on the brain and central nervous system. Developed at Russia's St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, it regulates inflammatory responses in the nervous system, restores balance between pro- and anti-oxidative processes, and stimulates interleukin-2 expression. Research shows potential benefits for ischemic brain injury recovery, nerve regeneration, and reducing autoimmune reactions affecting the CNS.
mechanism: Cortagen works through epigenetic regulation by penetrating cell nuclei and interacting with DNA to modulate gene expression. In the heart, it affects genes including Pass1, Hsc70, Bmp2, Wnt4, Eps15, and Eps15-rs. It powerfully regulates inflammatory responses in the nervous system, helping restore proper balance between oxidative and anti-oxidative processes. Cortagen stimulates IL-2 expression and helps regulate immune function, particularly by reducing autoimmune reactions.
keyBenefits: Modulates gene expression in neural tissue

items.name: Ischemic Brain Injury Recovery
items.description: Shows beneficial long-term results in research following ischemic brain injury.

items.name: Nerve Regeneration
items.description: Research demonstrates effects on restoration of injured nerve function.

items.name: Neuroinflammation
items.description: Regulates inflammatory response in the nervous system.

items.name: Autoimmune Modulation
items.description: Helps regulate immune function by reducing autoimmune reactions.

items.name: IL-2 Stimulation
items.description: Stimulates interleukin-2 expression for immune regulation.

items.name: Oxidative Balance
items.description: Restores balance between pro- and anti-oxidative processes.

items.name: Brain Aging
items.description: Addresses age-related CNS changes through bioregulation.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles, often repeated 2-3 times per year.
Injectable form available for direct administration, bypassing GI tract.

interactions.notes: Complementary CNS bioregulators; Cortagen for cortex, Pinealon for general brain.
Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Different organ targets; can be used in comprehensive bioregulator protocols.
Part of Khavinson bioregulator family; targets different tissue.

timeline.effects: Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

references.title: Effect of Cortagen on Gene Expression in Mouse Heart
references.keyFindings: Cortagen affects expression of genes including Pass1, Hsc70, Bmp2, Wnt4, Eps15 in heart tissue.

references.keyFindings: Demonstrated effects on nerve function restoration following injury.
